item.title: Ex-judges push to look into "anti-weaponization fund" deal
item.description: A group of 35 former federal judges asked a court to reopen a legal dispute between President Trump and the government, calling the deal to create a $1.776 billion "anti-weaponization fund" potentially fraudulent.

item.title: GPS data appears to contradict Brian Hooker's account of wife's disappearance
item.description: After forensic evidence appeared to contradict Brian Hooker's account of where Lynette Hooker was​ the night she disappeared, U.S. investigators are renewing their search in the Bahamas.

item.title: Spelling bee coach says, "everyone has this potential"
item.description: The 98th Scripps National Spelling Bee finals are on Thursday night. Tony Dokoupil has the story about one spelling bee coach's mission to "make every speller the best speller they can be."

item.title: New questions about Brian Hooker's account of wife's Bahamas disappearance
item.description: New forensic evidence appears to contradict Brian Hooker's account of where he says he was the night his wife Lynette went missing in the Bahamas. Cristian Benavides has the details.

item.title: Americans on health insurance: "I pay a lot of money for and it covers very little"
item.description: In the 1800s, Hartford, Connecticut, picked up the nickname, "The Insurance Capital of the World." Tony Dokoupil visits the city to ask people about rising insurance and healthcare costs.

item.title: U.S. carries out new strikes against Iranian military facility, official says
item.description: The U.S. military carried out another round of strikes on Iran, a U.S. official confirmed to CBS News on Wednesday, another challenge to a shaky ceasefire between the two countries.

item.title: Iran "negotiating on fumes," Trump says, as U.S. conducts new strikes
item.description: The U.S. and Iran had appeared ready to de-escalate before the U.S. military conducted what it said were defensive strikes against Iran on Wednesday.

item.title: Ken Paxton to face James Talarico in Texas Senate race
item.description: In Texas, the state's Attorney General Ken Paxton ousted four-term Senator John Cornyn, making him the third Republican incumbent to lose this month after failing to earn President Trump's endorsement. Ed O'Keefe spoke with Paxton's Democratic opponent, James Talarico.

item.title: 5 miners found alive in Laos caves after monsoon trapped them, 2 still missing
item.description: Five gold miners trapped inside a flooded cave in Laos were found alive, after being cut off for over a week. Matt Gutman has more on the mission.

item.title: New York City man sentenced to 10 years in plot to kill Iranian American writer
item.description: A federal judge has given a onetime truck and bus driver charged in an assassination plot against an Iranian American writer a 10-year prison sentence.

item.title: Matthew Perry's assistant sentenced over ketamine injection
item.description: "Friends" star Matthew Perry drowned in his hot tub in 2023 under the effects of ketamine. The 54-year-old's longtime personal assistant, who gave him the fatal injection, pleaded guilty to conspiracy and was sentenced to over three years in prison.

item.title: Trump's optimism on an Iran peace deal seems to waver
item.description: President Trump warned on Wednesday that even with control of Congress on the line in November, he'll wait out Tehran on a peace deal. As he put it, "I don't care about the midterms." Nancy Cordes has more.

item.title: Latest details on Washington state chemical tank rupture
item.description: Officials in Washington state are still working to recover nine bodies after a massive chemical tank at a paper mill ruptured on Tuesday. Jonathan Vigliotti reports on the latest details.

item.title: U.S. setting up Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya, officials say
item.description: The U.S. is in the process of setting up a quarantine facility in Kenya for Americans who were exposed to Ebola​ or infected with the deadly illness.

item.title: Uganda closes its border with Congo over Ebola outbreak
item.description: Uganda on Wednesday ordered the closure of its border with Congo, where suspected cases of a rare type of Ebola are surging.

item.title: Extended interview: Ben Rhodes on Iran, the state of the Democratic party, his new book and more
item.description: Ben Rhodes, former foreign policy adviser and speechwriter for President Obama, speaks with Major Garrett about the war in Iran, the deal the Obama administration made with Iran, his new book, "All We Say: A History of the United States in Fifteen Speeches" and more.

item.title: Top FEMA official Bob Fenton says "we're ready for hurricane season"
item.description: FEMA says it's ready for hurricane season, though it's still racing to recover from months of shutdown disruptions, delayed grants and a depleted Disaster Relief Fund.

item.title: FEMA administrator says they're ready for hurricane season: "It's in our DNA"
item.description: Next Monday marks the official start of the Atlantic hurricane season. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga spoke with Bob Fenton, FEMA's acting administrator, to discuss the administration's preparedness.

item.title: Takeaways from Ken Paxton's Texas runoff win over John Cornyn
item.description: Tuesday's runoff election in Texas saw another Republican incumbent lose his seat to a Trump-backed nominee. Four-term Sen. John Cornyn conceded the race to state Attorney General Ken Paxton, who picked up a late endorsement from President Trump. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has more from the Lone Star State.

item.title: Trump says Iran is "negotiating on fumes" and that he doesn't care about midterms
item.description: President Trump held a Cabinet meeting at the White House on Wednesday where he said talks with the Iranian regime are ongoing, but left the door open to resume fighting if necessary. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Charlie D'Agata have more.

item.title: What to make of Trump administration's latest comments on Iran
item.description: White House officials spoke about the war in Iran during a Cabinet meeting with President Trump on Wednesday. CBS News national security contributor Joseph Zacks has more.

item.title: Trump visited soldiers at Walter Reed — but not the 14 injured in the Iran war
item.description: More than a dozen soldiers injured in Operation Epic Fury are still recovering at the military hospital.

item.title: James Talarico on past comments about God, Senate race with Ken Paxton, more
item.description: Democrat James Talarico will face off with Republican Ken Paxton for a U.S. Senate seat after Paxton beat Sen. John Cornyn in Texas' GOP primary runoff Wednesday night. Talarico spoke with Ed O'Keefe about his campaign.

item.title: Takeaways from Texas' primaries: Trump's power, Latino vote, redistricting and more
item.description: The November ballot in Texas is now set, after a four-term Republican senator lost to a Trump-endorsed challenger in Tuesday's primary runoffs. Meanwhile, Texas Democrats are angling to win their first Senate race since 1988.

item.title: 2 dead, 9 workers "unrecovered" after Washington chemical tank implosion
item.description: The damaged tank at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. held approximately 900,000 gallons of white liquor, a chemical used in paper processing, authorities said.

item.title: YouTube taking steps to make clear when realistic videos are made by AI
item.description: YouTube said it will automatically label photorealistic content created by AI, the video platform said.

item.title: Conservative tech critic seen as leading candidate to be DOJ antitrust chief
item.description: The former assistant attorney general for the Justice Department Antitrust Division, Gail Slater, was abruptly terminated in February after clashes with Pam Bondi and Todd Blanche.

item.title: Matthew Perry's assistant who injected him with fatal dose of ketamine gets over 3 years in prison
item.description: Kenneth Iwamasa, 60, is the fifth and final person sentenced for playing a role in Matthew Perry's 2023 ketamine death.

item.title: How much money you need to earn to afford a home in 49 U.S. cities
item.description: A household earning the average income would need to spend 40% of its income to afford the typical U.S. home, according to Redfin.

item.title: Fire at kosher store in London "non-suspicious," police say
item.description: London police say a fire at a kosher store in a London neighborhood with a large Jewish community that's been targeted repeatedly is "non-suspicious."
